attacking_style: Long-range breakaway specialist capable of sustaining efforts from 48km out, targeting stages with multiple climbs where GC teams may allow freedom. Thrives when given protected freedom rather than domestique duties.
team_tactics: Granted freedom to join breakaways on stages where GC ambitions don't conflict. No visible team support in breakaway execution, suggesting opportunistic rider liberation rather than planned team setups.
terrain_preference: Multi-climb stages suited to sustained efforts over 40+ kilometers. Benefits from terrain difficult enough to discourage peloton chase but not steep enough to favor pure climbers in breakaway selections.
vulnerability: Limited data, but 35-year age and first Grand Tour stage win suggests historically constrained by team domestique role. May lack explosiveness for short, punchy finishes or steep gradient attacks.
key_patterns: Capable of 40+ kilometer solo or small group efforts on mountainous terrain.
Career breakthrough at 35 suggests late-blooming or newly granted tactical freedom.
Monument winner with stage-hunting capability when released from support duties.
form_trajectory: positive: First Grand Tour stage win at 35 following Monument victory indicates career-best form and newfound freedom to target personal results
